heading: Clearing for security-based swaps
label: 78c–3 Clearing for security-based swaps

children.heading: Standard for clearing
children.label: 1 Standard for clearing
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any person to engage in a security-based swap unless that person submits such security-based swap for clearing to a clearing agency that is registered under this chapter or a clearing agency that is exempt from registration under this chapter if the security-based swap is required to be cleared.

children.label: A
children.text: prescribe that all security-based swaps submitted to the clearing agency with the same terms and conditions are economically equivalent within the clearing agency and may be offset with each other within the clearing agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide for non-discriminatory clearing of a security-based swap executed bilaterally or on or through the rules of an unaffiliated national securities exchange or security-based swap execution facility.

children.label: A
children.text: The Commission on an ongoing basis shall review each security-based swap, or any group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps to make a determination that such security-based swap, or group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps should be required to be cleared.

children.label: B
children.text: The Commission shall provide at least a 30-day public comment period regarding any determination under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: A clearing agency shall submit to the Commission each security-based swap, or any group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps that it plans to accept for clearing and provide notice to its members (in a manner to be determined by the Commission) of such submission.

children.label: B
children.text: Any security-based swap or group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps listed for clearing by a clearing agency as of , shall be considered submitted to the Commission.July 21, 2010

children.label: i
children.text: make available to the public any submission received under subparagraphs (A) and (B);

children.label: ii
children.text: review each submission made under subparagraphs (A) and (B), and determine whether the security-based swap, or group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps, described in the submission is required to be cleared; and

children.label: iii
children.text: provide at least a 30-day public comment period regarding its determination whether the clearing requirement under subsection (a)(1) shall apply to the submission.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 3 Deadline
children.text: The Commission shall make its determination under paragraph (2)(C) not later than 90 days after receiving a submission made under paragraphs (2)(A) and (2)(B), unless the submitting clearing agency agrees to an extension for the time limitation established under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: In reviewing a submission made under paragraph (2), the Commission shall review whether the submission is consistent with .section 78q–1 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: The existence of significant outstanding notional exposures, trading liquidity and adequate pricing data.

children.label: ii
children.text: The availability of rule framework, capacity, operational expertise and resources, and credit support infrastructure to clear the contract on terms that are consistent with the material terms and trading conventions on which the contract is then traded.

children.label: iii
children.text: The effect on the mitigation of systemic risk, taking into account the size of the market for such contract and the resources of the clearing agency available to clear the contract.

children.label: iv
children.text: The effect on competition, including appropriate fees and charges applied to clearing.

children.label: v
children.text: The existence of reasonable legal certainty in the event of the insolvency of the relevant clearing agency or 1 or more of its clearing members with regard to the treatment of customer and security-based swap counterparty positions, funds, and property.

children.label: C
children.text: In making a determination under subsection (b)(1) or paragraph (2)(C) that the clearing requirement shall apply, the Commission may require such terms and conditions to the requirement as the Commission determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 5 Rules
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Commission shall adopt rules for a clearing agency’s submission for review, pursuant to this subsection, of a security-based swap, or a group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps, that it seeks to accept for clearing. Nothing in this paragraph limits the Commission from making a determination under paragraph (2)(C) for security-based swaps described in paragraph (2)(B).July 21, 2010

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: After making a determination pursuant to subsection (b)(2), the Commission, on application of a counterparty to a security-based swap or on its own initiative, may stay the clearing requirement of subsection (a)(1) until the Commission completes a review of the terms of the security-based swap (or the group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps) and the clearing arrangement.

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 2 Deadline
children.text: The Commission shall complete a review undertaken pursuant to paragraph (1) not later than 90 days after issuance of the stay, unless the clearing agency that clears the security-based swap, or group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps, agrees to an extension of the time limitation established under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: determine, unconditionally or subject to such terms and conditions as the Commission determines to be appropriate, that the security-based swap, or group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps, must be cleared pursuant to this subsection if it finds that such clearing is consistent with subsection (b)(4); or

children.label: B
children.text: determine that the clearing requirement of subsection (a)(1) shall not apply to the security-based swap, or group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 4 Rules
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Commission shall adopt rules for reviewing, pursuant to this subsection, a clearing agency’s clearing of a security-based swap, or a group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps, that it has accepted for clearing.July 21, 2010

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall prescribe rules under this section (and issue interpretations of rules prescribed under this section), as determined by the Commission to be necessary to prevent evasions of the mandatory clearing requirements under this chapter.

children.heading: Duty of Commission to investigate and take certain actions
children.label: 2 Duty of Commission to investigate and take certain actions
A
children.text: investigate the relevant facts and circumstances;

children.label: B
children.text: within 30 days issue a public report containing the results of the investigation; and

children.label: C
children.text: take such actions as the Commission determines to be necessary and in the public interest, which may include requiring the retaining of adequate margin or capital by parties to the security-based swap or the group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps.

children.label: A
children.text: authorizes the Commission to adopt rules requiring a clearing agency to list for clearing a security-based swap or any group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps if the clearing of the security-based swap or the group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps would threaten the financial integrity of the clearing agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: affects the authority of the Commission to enforce the open access provisions of subsection (a)(2) with respect to a security-based swap or the group, category, type, or class of security-based swaps that is listed for clearing by a clearing agency.

Security-based swaps entered into before , shall be reported to a registered security-based swap data repository or the Commission no later than 180 days after the effective date of this section.July 21, 2010

children.label: A
children.text: 90 days after such effective date; or

children.label: B
children.text: such other time after entering into the security-based swap as the Commission may prescribe by rule or regulation.
Security-based swaps entered into before , are exempt from the clearing requirements of this subsection if reported pursuant to subsection (e)(1).July 21, 2010

Security-based swaps entered into before application of the clearing requirement pursuant to this section are exempt from the clearing requirements of this section if reported pursuant to subsection (e)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: is not a financial entity;

children.label: B
children.text: is using security-based swaps to hedge or mitigate commercial risk; and

children.label: C
children.text: notifies the Commission, in a manner set forth by the Commission, how it generally meets its financial obligations associated with entering into non-cleared security-based swaps.

children.heading: Option to clear
children.label: 2 Option to clear
children.text: The application of the clearing exception in paragraph (1) is solely at the discretion of the counterparty to the security-based swap that meets the conditions of subparagraphs (A) through (C) of paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: a swap dealer;

children.label: ii
children.text: a security-based swap dealer;

children.label: iii
children.text: a major swap participant;

children.label: iv
children.text: a major security-based swap participant;

children.label: v
children.text: a commodity pool as defined in ;section 1a(10) of title 7

children.label: vi
children.text: a private fund as defined in ;section 80b–2(a) of this title

children.label: vii
children.text: an employee benefit plan as defined in paragraphs (3) and (32) of ;section 1002 of title 29

children.label: viii
children.text: a person predominantly engaged in activities that are in the business of banking or financial in nature, as defined in .section 1843(k) of title 12

children.label: i
children.text: depository institutions with total assets of $10,000,000,000 or less;

children.label: ii
children.text: farm credit system institutions with total assets of $10,000,000,000 or less; or

children.label: iii
children.text: credit unions with total assets of $10,000,000,000 or less.

children.label: i
children.text: enters into the security-based swap to hedge or mitigate the commercial risk of the person or other affiliate of the person that is not a financial entity, and the commercial risk that the affiliate is hedging or mitigating has been transferred to the affiliate;

children.label: ii
children.text: is directly and wholly-owned by another affiliate qualified for the exception under this paragraph or an entity that is not a financial entity;

children.label: iii
children.text: is not indirectly majority-owned by a financial entity;

children.label: iv
children.text: is not ultimately owned by a parent company that is a financial entity; and

children.label: v
children.text: does not provide any services, financial or otherwise, to any affiliate that is a nonbank financial company supervised by the Board of Governors (as defined under ).section 5311 of title 12

children.label: i
children.text: a swap dealer;

children.label: ii
children.text: a security-based swap dealer;

children.label: iii
children.text: a major swap participant;

children.label: iv
children.text: a major security-based swap participant;

children.label: v
children.text: a commodity pool;

children.label: vi
children.text: a bank holding company;

children.label: vii
children.text: a private fund, as defined in ;section 80b–2(a) of this title

children.label: viii
children.text: an employee benefit plan or government plan, as defined in paragraphs (3) and (32) of ;1section 1002 of title 29So in original. Probably should be “governmental”.1

children.label: ix
children.text: an insured depository institution;

children.label: x
children.text: a farm credit system institution;

children.label: xi
children.text: a credit union;

children.label: xii
children.text: a nonbank financial company supervised by the Board of Governors (as defined under ); orsection 5311 of title 12

children.label: xiii
children.text: an entity engaged in the business of insurance and subject to capital requirements established by an insurance governmental authority of a State, a territory of the United States, the District of Columbia, a country other than the United States, or a political subdivision of a country other than the United States that is engaged in the supervision of insurance companies under insurance law.

children.label: i
children.text: a major security-based swap participant;

children.label: ii
children.text: a security-based swap dealer;

children.label: iii
children.text: a major swap participant; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a swap dealer.

children.label: i
children.text: such affiliate may not enter into any security-based swap other than for the purpose of hedging or mitigating commercial risk; and

children.label: ii
children.text: neither such affiliate nor any person affiliated with such affiliate that is not a financial entity may enter into a security-based swap with or on behalf of any affiliate that is a financial entity or otherwise assume, net, combine, or consolidate the risk of security-based swaps entered into by any such financial entity, except one that is an affiliate that qualifies for the exception under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Transition rule for affiliates
children.label: E Transition rule for affiliates
children.text: An affiliate, subsidiary, or a wholly owned entity of a person that qualifies for an exception under subparagraph (A) and is predominantly engaged in providing financing for the purchase or lease of merchandise or manufactured goods of the person shall be exempt from the margin requirement described in section 78–10(e) of this title and the clearing requirement described in subsection (a) with regard to security-based swaps entered into to mitigate the risk of the financing activities for not less than a 2-year period beginning on .oJuly 21, 2010

children.heading: Risk management program
children.label: F Risk management program
children.text: Any security-based swap entered into by an affiliate that qualifies for the exception in subparagraph (A) shall be subject to a centralized risk management program of the affiliate, which is reasonably designed both to monitor and manage the risks associated with the security-based swap and to identify each of the affiliates on whose behalf a security-based swap was entered into.

children.heading: Security-based swaps required to be cleared
children.label: A Security-based swaps required to be cleared
children.text: With respect to any security-based swap that is subject to the mandatory clearing requirement under subsection (a) and entered into by a security-based swap dealer or a major security-based swap participant with a counterparty that is not a swap dealer, major swap participant, security-based swap dealer, or major security-based swap participant, the counterparty shall have the sole right to select the clearing agency at which the security-based swap will be cleared.

children.heading: Security-based swaps not required to be cleared
children.label: B Security-based swaps not required to be cleared
i
children.text: may elect to require clearing of the security-based swap; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall have the sole right to select the clearing agency at which the security-based swap will be cleared.

children.heading: Abuse of exception
children.label: 6 Abuse of exception
children.text: The Commission may prescribe such rules or issue interpretations of the rules as the Commission determines to be necessary to prevent abuse of the exceptions described in this subsection. The Commission may also request information from those persons claiming the clearing exception as necessary to prevent abuse of the exceptions described in this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: execute the transaction on an exchange; or

children.label: B
children.text: execute the transaction on a security-based swap execution facility registered under or a security-based swap execution facility that is exempt from registration under .section 78c–4 of this titlesection 78c–4(e) of this title

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: The requirements of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1) shall not apply if no exchange or security-based swap execution facility makes the security-based swap available to trade or for security-based swap transactions subject to the clearing exception under subsection (g).

children.heading: Board approval
children.label: i Board approval
children.text: Exemptions from the requirements of this section to clear a security-based swap or execute a security-based swap through a national securities exchange or security-based swap execution facility shall be available to a counterparty that is an issuer of securities that are registered under section 78 of this title or that is required to file reports pursuant to section 78(d) of this title, only if an appropriate committee of the issuer’s board or governing body has reviewed and approved the issuer’s decision to enter into security-based swaps that are subject to such exemptions.lo

children.heading: Designation of chief compliance officer
children.label: j Designation of chief compliance officer

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each registered clearing agency shall designate an individual to serve as a chief compliance officer.

children.label: A
children.text: report directly to the board or to the senior officer of the clearing agency;

children.label: B
children.text: in consultation with its board, a body performing a function similar thereto, or the senior officer of the registered clearing agency, resolve any conflicts of interest that may arise;

children.label: C
children.text: be responsible for administering each policy and procedure that is required to be established pursuant to this section;

children.label: D
children.text: ensure compliance with this chapter (including regulations issued under this chapter) relating to agreements, contracts, or transactions, including each rule prescribed by the Commission under this section;

children.label: i
children.text: compliance office review;

children.label: ii
children.text: look-back;

children.label: iii
children.text: internal or external audit finding;

children.label: iv
children.text: self-reported error; or

children.label: v
children.text: validated complaint; and

children.label: F
children.text: establish and follow appropriate procedures for the handling, management response, remediation, retesting, and closing of noncompliance issues.

children.label: i
children.text: the compliance of the registered clearing agency or security-based swap execution facility of the compliance officer with respect to this chapter (including regulations under this chapter); and

children.label: ii
children.text: each policy and procedure of the registered clearing agency of the compliance officer (including the code of ethics and conflict of interest policies of the registered clearing agency).

children.label: i
children.text: accompany each appropriate financial report of the registered clearing agency that is required to be furnished to the Commission pursuant to this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include a certification that, under penalty of law, the compliance report is accurate and complete.
